source_pr.title: planner: try to fix the unstable test `TestAnalyzeIncremental`
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #25187, #25010 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary: planner: try to fix the unstable test `TestAnalyzeIncremental`

### What is changed and how it works?

I'm not sure if this PR can fix the unstable test, but it seems that these two issues are caused by we don't load/update statistics on time after inserting some data.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- `No release note`

symptoms: planner: try to fix the unstable test `TestAnalyzeIncremental`
failure_signature: planner: try to fix the unstable test `TestAnalyzeIncremental`
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to timing/eventual-consistency issues in statistics state: after updates/analyze operations, required histograms may not be loaded/ready when assertions run. This PR stabilizes it by explicitly calling LoadNeededHistograms() at key points before asserting on show stats_buckets and table stats.
fix_pattern: Call LoadNeededHistograms() before asserting stats buckets (executor/analyze_test.go)

evidence: evidence: planner: try to fix the unstable test `TestAnalyzeIncremental`
